vitals.participation_trend: Stable at ~5000 riders, 5th largest in France

course_description.overview: Challenging road cyclosportive through Ariège Pyrenees featuring iconic climbs like Col de Pailhères and Plateau de Beille, starting and finishing in Tarascon-sur-Ariège.[1][3]

key_segments.name: Plateau de Beille
key_segments.description: Steep finish climb used in Tour de France.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Brutal Pyrenean climbs with 4500+m elevation over 170km make it a top-tier gran fondo, demanding strong climbing ability.[1][3]

logistics.getting_there: Accessible by car via Toulouse (1.5h) or Toulouse-Blagnac Airport; train to nearby Foix then local transport.
logistics.accommodation: Book early in Tarascon-sur-Ariège; campsites, hotels, and guesthouses in prehistoric valley area.
logistics.registration_details: Online pre-registration with dossier review for XXL; main event inscriptions open earlier, bib pickup Friday June 26 9AM-8PM or Saturday early morning.[1][5]
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes required; GPX tracks provided for XXL; mechanic services likely at start/finish.
logistics.local_considerations: Expect traffic on open roads; hot June weather in Pyrenees; prehistoric sites nearby for tourism.[2]

final_verdict.one_liner: Pyrenean climbing showcase, France's 5th largest gran fondo.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road climbers seeking Tour de France-style cols with big-field energy.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for epic scenery and prestige in accessible Pyrenees location.
pro_tips: Train for 4500m+ climbs; book accommodation 6 months ahead.
Prioritize early bib pickup on Friday.
Use GPX for navigation on open roads.
Combine with VTT or XXL ultra for full weekend.
